content:
Only information explicitly present in the approved knowledge base may be stated as a Data X Technologies fact.

• Never invent or estimate prices, starting prices, discounts, payment plans, project durations, guarantees, rankings, staff capacity, addresses, contact details, availability, clients, projects, testimonials, packages, or promotions.
• A quotation can be discussed only as a scope-dependent process; no amount is confirmed in this knowledge base.
• A timeline can be discussed only after requirements and technical scope are reviewed; no duration is confirmed here.
• SEO results and rankings must never be guaranteed.
• If a requested fact is absent, say it is not available in the approved information and offer to collect requirements or arrange follow-up through an officially configured channel.
• Do not claim that a meeting, callback, WhatsApp message, or email has been scheduled or sent unless the connected business system confirms it.

Data X Technologies is presented in the approved project material as a provider of business software and digital services.

Confirmed service categories are:

• Custom software development
• Website design and development
• Mobile application development
• Customer relationship management systems
• Business and management systems
• Business-process automation
• Search engine optimization
• Digital marketing

The approved material does not provide a legal-registration description, establishment date, team size, office address, business hours, official contact details, certifications, partnerships, or guarantees.

document_title: Contact, address, hours, meetings, and availability
content:
The current approved knowledge base does not contain an official phone number, WhatsApp number, email address, website URL, office address, business hours, timezone, consultation schedule, or booking link.

Do not provide a guessed contact method or claim that a message or appointment has been created. A deployment may expose contact or scheduling details only after they are supplied by an authorized representative and added to this document.

When asked for contact or a meeting, collect the customer's preferred contact method and preferred time, then state that confirmation depends on the connected business team or scheduling system.

Data X Technologies is associated with custom software, websites, mobile applications, CRM systems, business and management systems, business automation, SEO, and digital marketing.

Service details are determined from the customer's business problem and required scope. Features mentioned during discovery are possible requirement categories, not promises of inclusion. Final deliverables, integrations, technology, price, support, and schedule require official review and quotation.

The knowledge base contains no approved packages or fixed bundles.

document_title: Custom software, business systems, and management systems
content:
Custom software and management systems are considered when a business has workflows that generic tools do not adequately support.

Problems that may be explored during requirements discovery include repeated manual entry, disconnected records, manual reporting, task follow-up, inventory visibility, order tracking, billing workflows, access control, and coordination between teams. These are discovery examples, not confirmed features in every project.

Possible requirement areas include users and roles, workflow steps, records, reports, notifications, approvals, integrations, data migration, backups, security expectations, and administrative controls. Inclusion depends on approved scope.

Suitable organizations may include businesses with repeatable operational processes or a need to centralize information. Suitability must be assessed from actual requirements.

Website design and development is a confirmed service category. Discovery may cover a new website or improvements to an existing website.

Common problems to assess include an outdated design, poor mobile usability, slow pages, difficult content updates, unclear navigation, missing enquiry flows, or a need for online ordering. These are potential problems, not claims that every project includes their solution.

Possible requirements include page count, content, branding, responsive design, administration, forms, product or service listings, ordering, payments, integrations, hosting, migration, analytics, and maintenance. Each item requires scope confirmation.

An existing website may be reviewed for improvement, but feasibility depends on its platform, access, condition, and requested changes.

Mobile application development is a confirmed service category. No particular platform, framework, app-store service, or feature set is guaranteed by the current knowledge base.

Discovery should identify the users, business purpose, target devices, required workflows, login and roles, data handled, notifications, payments, location or device capabilities, integrations, administration needs, and whether an existing backend is available.

A mobile application may be suitable when customers or staff need frequent, device-focused access to a business workflow. The choice between a mobile app and a responsive website requires requirements review.

CRM systems are a confirmed service category. A CRM requirement generally concerns organized customer, lead, communication, or follow-up information.

Discovery may cover lead sources, customer records, pipeline stages, assignments, notes, reminders, follow-ups, reporting, permissions, imports, and integrations. These are possible requirement areas and are not a promised standard package.

A CRM may be useful when customer information is scattered, follow-ups are missed, ownership is unclear, or reporting is manual. The specific solution depends on the organization's workflow and approved scope.

SEO and digital marketing are confirmed service categories. The current knowledge base does not specify channels, packages, ad-spend arrangements, deliverables, reporting frequency, or prices.

Discovery may consider business goals, target audience, locations, current website, existing accounts, competitors, campaign history, content resources, measurement access, and budget approved by the customer.

No search ranking, lead volume, sales result, or campaign outcome is guaranteed. Recommendations and scope require review of the current digital presence and objectives.

A quotation should be prepared only after the relevant scope is understood. Useful inputs include:

• Business type and the problem to solve
• Intended users and their roles
• Required workflows and highest-priority features
• Existing website, system, data, or vendor
• Required integrations
• Content, branding, and language needs
• Target platform or devices, if already decided
• Security, access, reporting, and data-migration needs
• Desired timing as a customer preference, not a confirmed delivery promise
• Budget range if the customer chooses to provide it
• Examples or references that clarify expectations

Price may be affected by scope, complexity, number of workflows or screens, integrations, data migration, content, design requirements, platforms, testing, deployment, and support. This list explains quotation inputs; it does not authorize an estimated amount.

No confirmed starting prices, packages, payment plans, or project durations are present in the knowledge base.
The confirmed categories are custom software, website design and development, mobile applications, CRM systems, business and management systems, business automation, SEO, and digital marketing.

A quotation requires review of the business problem, required features, users, integrations, existing systems or data, and other delivery requirements. The relevant team must approve the final scope and amount.

section: How long can a project take?
content: No standard or confirmed duration is present in the knowledge base. Timing depends on approved scope and must be confirmed after requirements review.

section: What information is needed from the customer?
content: The customer should describe the business, problem, intended users, priority features, current tools, required integrations, available content or data, and desired outcome.

section: Can an existing website or system be updated?
content: An existing solution may be reviewed. Feasibility depends on its technology, access, current condition, and requested changes; it cannot be promised before review.
No approved price or starting price is available. A factual quotation requires confirmed requirements and official review.
No approved payment-plan information is present.

document_title: Portfolio, company proof, promotions, and current information
content:
The current approved knowledge base contains no authorized project list, portfolio links, client names, case studies, testimonials, certifications, awards, or other company proof.

It also contains no current promotions, offers, discounts, service-availability notices, consultation slots, or campaign expiry dates.

Do not invent examples or identify a customer without explicit approval. When proof or current offers are requested, state that approved information is not presently available in the knowledge base and request follow-up from the authorized business team.

Acknowledge the concern. Explain that no amount should be guessed before scope review and that priorities can be identified for an official quotation. Do not promise a discount, phase plan, or payment plan unless officially approved.
Explain that timing depends on confirmed scope, dependencies, content, integrations, testing, and approvals. Do not provide an unsupported duration.

Respect the existing relationship. The customer may first ask the current vendor to address the issue. Data X Technologies can discuss a review only if that service and review process are officially available; do not criticize another provider.

section: “Why might a custom system be useful?”
content: A custom system may be considered when existing tools do not fit important workflows, information is duplicated, reporting is manual, or teams need a shared process. Suitability requires requirements review.

Improvement may be considered when users face mobile, speed, navigation, content-management, enquiry, ordering, accessibility, or integration problems. The actual issue should be checked before recommending work.

Keep objection responses informative and calm. Do not use pressure, false urgency, guarantees, or unsupported comparisons.
